entry.title: Start Here!
entry.content:
The hardest decision I made this year was to leave my Kung Fu Family and go on a new adventure without any guarantee what the future might bring. This year, I am traveling the country to study Parkour, camp and volunteer exchange. The hope is to acquire the skills I need to build my monastery, expand my family and gain confidence with my ability to network and generate income as a web developer, A.I Programmer and writer.

In 2020 we'll spend some time traveling the states, and then a few months in Canada. If little fig gets accepted into Spanish Language Immersion school, there will be slight modifications but no changes. After 2020 if all goes well we plan to travel abroad throughout Europe and possibly South America for 3 years. Our hope between 2021 and 2023 is to purchase land in the U.S and at the end of the 3 years purchase land abroad where we hope to live 6 Months working to acquire residency, and 3-6 Months in the USA.


March Update:

March Update: At the end of February I was assaulted in a road rage incident. An old black Toyota Corolla without a license plate perceived I cut them off and deliberately rammed into my Prius. Fortunately no one but me was in the vehicle and we were covered by good insurance. The car went into the shop at the start of March and will be out at the end of the week. I am continuing to prepare for our original journey within the USA and abroad in Canada, but there's been a slight modification.

The thing that bothered me the most, wasn't that the vehicle hit me. It was that the woman who hit me and wasn't held accountable for her actions. It bothers me that in our society we can hurt other people and for the most part if you don't know your assailant the chances of them getting away with it is pretty high. The officer I spoke to recommended I get a gun to protect me and my child, and I thought about it. And while I will never carry a gun in my house after weighing all possible scenarios I could think of, I did decide that I will receive tactical training and want to be someone who helps solve crimes and bring people who hurt others to justice. For 72 hours I played with the idea of becoming a volunteer police officer, EMT, and 911 Dispatcher. After the first 48 hours I realized that I am unable to dedicate the fulltime required to be a 911 Dispatcher and that I am not compatible for being an American Police Officer. Though there is still opportunity for me to pursue Volunteer EMT.

My hope is now that I can find a country that aligns with my values and become an Officer and 911 Dispatcher in that country. And then to use that experience to volunteer at places like O.U.R. Rescue and/or similar, bringing those skills and experiences back to my own Guardian Monastery. Should I fail to do even that my hope falls on utilizing the Guardian Monastery to fill a void in providing at a minimum aftercare. There's a special place in my heart for pedophiles, rapists, and people who torture and hurt others. I hate that we live in a world where such people exist. More than anything I aim to be part of the solution in making the world a brighter place, not just in the way I live but in this world where it feels like the people who hurt others outnumber those who don't.


See about to see the overview of skills I am learning on my journey.
entry.summary: The hardest decision I made this year was to leave my Kung Fu Family and go on a new adventure without any guarantee what the future might bring. This year, I am traveling the country to study Parkour, camp and volunteer exchange. The hope is to acquire the skills I need to build my monastery, expand my family and gain confidence with my ability to network and generate income as a web developer, A.I Programmer and writer.

entry.title: Mealworms
entry.content:
I became interested in mealworms when I learned they can compost styrofoam. I’ve only had them for a few months and already their population has exploded, far faster than the roaches. I hear their waste isn’t that profitable, but who knows. If it isn’t I’ll just experiment and add it to the worm bin, and if it is, then the frass of the mealworms alongside the mealworms would be a good way to generate income for the monastery.

I have three types of mealworms. Buffalo Beetles (Alphitobius diaperinus), Mealworms (Tenebrio Molitor), and Superworms (Zophobos morios). The Superworms are large and so aggressive that they will eat even each other, because of this they will only pupate if no other superworms are present. The mealworms eat styrofoam just like the superworms, but aren't as aggressive eaters as them either. And the Bufflo Beetles are gentler than the mealworms, enough to be placed into the Ivory tank as a cleaner (they won't eat molting Ivory's)

I've overall decided that as useful as the superworms are, they are just too high maintenance. I will not be breeding them and will stick to working with Tenebrio Molitor and Alphitobius Diaperinus until I can come up with an alternative solution for dealing with the superworms (such as separate compartments for each mealworm and then feeding them until they turn into beetles). In the future I hope to be able to work with Dung Beetles to see if they can compost and breakdown human waste. And at a later date Black Soldier Flies. All my composting insects attract fruit flies, and so I'm working on aggressively combatting them.
entry.summary: I became interested in mealworms when I learned they can compost styrofoam. I’ve only had them for a few months and already their population has exploded, far faster than the roaches. I hear their waste isn’t that profitable, but who knows. If it isn’t I’ll just experiment and add it to the worm bin, and if it is, then the frass of the mealworms alongside the mealworms would be a good way to generate income for the monastery.

entry.title: Blatticomposting
entry.content:
8 months ago beginning in 2019 I decided to begin composting with Roaches. Ivory Roaches sell more than Red Wrigglers do, are more forgiving when you make a mistake, eat more and require less than my worms. They have overall been really enjoyable to work with.

In 2020 my focus is getting my colony to high enough numbers where I can compost waste, and then sell the excess Ivory Head's and compost under a Guardian & Hero structure at food markets and stands part-time. I'm also looking into potentially taking them with me in my domestic travels.
entry.summary: 8 months ago beginning in 2019 I decided to begin composting with Roaches. Ivory Roaches sell more than Red Wrigglers do, are more forgiving when you make a mistake, eat more and require less than my worms. They have overall been really enjoyable to work with.

entry.title: Japanese Bed
entry.content:
I made this camping bed out of an old japanese roll-up futon, a deflated lightspeed bed, an eggshell foam matt inserted into the lightspeed bed (for waterproofing), and that’s it. The Sleeping bag serves as a really warm blanket that has been with me since 2014. My only problem with this bed is that if it’s placed directly on the floor of the camp or children pee on it it won’t be easily washable. So my next goal is to find a low-cost solution to wrap it up. My other challenge is that it’s not very great for backpacking in places where you can’t take your car – so it’s great for car camping, but not for outdoors.

I think if I can store it well enough, this and my teton cot brought back in 2014 might actually be a good combo, so long as it doesn't take too much space in the prius.
entry.summary: I made this camping bed out of an old japanese roll-up futon, a deflated lightspeed bed, an eggshell foam matt inserted into the lightspeed bed (for waterproofing), and that’s it. The Sleeping bag serves as a really warm blanket that has been with me since 2014. My only problem with this bed is that if it’s placed directly on the floor of the camp or children pee on it it won’t be easily washable. So my next goal is to find a low-cost solution to wrap it up. My other challenge is that it’s not very great for backpacking in places where you can’t take your car – so it’s great for car camping, but not for outdoors.

entry.title: Bell Tent
entry.content:
I brought my bell tent a year after building my yurt. Unfortunately, the opportunity to open the bell tent didn’t happen and due to flooding in the basement part of the canvas it was stored in molded. It didn’t effect the interior since the canvas of the tent was rolled inside the groundsheet so that the water never penetrated the canvas.

I first pitched the tent in Spring 2018. I’ve yet to master pitching the bell tent so that it looks like the picture, but Caitlyn from Stout bell tents was really helpful during the first seasonal pitch, and is continuing to help me now in troubleshooting long distance how to resolve the problems I have. The images shown here are the first pitch, flooding, and cleaning issues. In terms of camping, it doesn’t feel practical for short-term camping. So the plan in 2020 is to use the bell tent for long term camping, for stays up to a week, and use the prius for short term camping.

Most of my challenge is folding up the groundtent, esp. if it has rained for the week. Drying and getting the Bell Tent groundsheet clean without a bottom tarp was and continues to be a a challenge. During our first pitch we had to take the tent down and store it dirty due to rain, but brought it outside as soon as it stopped and began working on the ground tarp. There were a lot of grass stains. After getting it as clean as we could get it, we couldn't figure out how to fold it the way they had folded it to get it back in the actual duffel bag. That and it was too heavy to put the top and the bottom together again. So we put the canvas in the duffel bag and stored the groundsheet folded up. This and testing zipper integrity makes for an additional challenge if we're going to camp with the bell tent.
entry.summary: I brought my bell tent a year after building my yurt. Unfortunately, the opportunity to open the bell tent didn’t happen and due to flooding in the basement part of the canvas it was stored in molded. It didn’t effect the interior since the canvas of the tent was rolled inside the groundsheet so that the water never penetrated the canvas.

entry.title: Mycelium Bricks
entry.content:
I was particularly interested in mycelium’s ability to break down waste like cardboard and manure, and that some species could even break down plastic. In addition to that I saw incredible potential in using the mycelium as a sustainable building material that could be grown onsite. With the added bonus of being able to supply food like lion’s mane and king oyster once building was complete.

During the Summer I got a job at a cafe and one of the perks was a lot of coffee grounds. I tried my hand at pasteruizing and growing my own mycelium. But there was too much moisture in the bags and it quickly got overtaken by mold. I tried again with manure I had let dry out – but even with using a pressure cooker, nothing grew. I did get a TON of Red Wrigglers that I added to my worm bin, but no mycelium.

My goal is to live a lifestyle that converts all waste into a product that can be used. Mushrooms, Roaches, Worms, Black Soldier Flies, Butterflies, Dung Beetles and other critters that eat waste are a key component to this. Unfortunately the mushroom project was short lived. Financially between investing in the garden both indoors and outdoors, expanding the roaches, the candy and everything else -- dealing with the mycelium became another challenge. I plan to tackle this challenge again, but am not sure if I will do it this year or somewhere down the road.
entry.summary: I was particularly interested in mycelium’s ability to break down waste like cardboard and manure, and that some species could even break down plastic. In addition to that I saw incredible potential in using the mycelium as a sustainable building material that could be grown onsite. With the added bonus of being able to supply food like lion’s mane and king oyster once building was complete.

entry.title: Candy Detour
entry.content:
For several months I tried to make candy for a Guardian Hero fundraiser. The plan was to create little packages of lego candy and lollipops that were healthy and went part of gratitude packages for and from people who were either sick or struggling from mental illness or addictions, the latter being in recovery. It was meant to be healthy and guilt-free. Each note would be handwritten on aged paper, with a waxxed seal. For the kids there would be little positive notes reminding them how worthy and valuable they are in a single phrase, a bit like fortune cookies, but more like, gratitude cookies.

It turns out it is really freaking hard to make candy. I spent 3 months investing the equipment and trying to make healthy candies that looked and tasted good. After a lot of wasted candy, I finally did figure out how to make a good solid gummy. The only problem? It tasted nasty. So we switched to chocolate. It was easier to buy chocolate from sustainable sources and melt it down. The other problem? Aside from having to learn techniques to make good chocolate. Dark chocolate is healthier than white chocolate, and only white chocolate can be dyed.

In the end after all the experimentation I became fascinated by sugar sculpting. It was easy to melt down sugar, and while impossible to make a truly healthy lollipop the beauty of what could be created -- edible glass art was too hard to pass up.

To fund the project I began working at a cafe. I realized the whole point of working at the cafe was to fund the candy, which in turn was meant to fund my Guardian Hero Project...And then I thought, why not just use the income I have for the project itself. So, while working at the cafe I became distracted by the original composting projects and decided to revisit this one later. I actually loved this detour a lot, and still feel there is potential for sugar sculpting to become a hobby of mine in a few years time.
entry.summary: For several months I tried to make candy for a Guardian Hero fundraiser. The plan was to create little packages of lego candy and lollipops that were healthy and went part of gratitude packages for and from people who were either sick or struggling from mental illness or addictions, the latter being in recovery. It was meant to be healthy and guilt-free. Each note would be handwritten on aged paper, with a waxxed seal. For the kids there would be little positive notes reminding them how worthy and valuable they are in a single phrase, a bit like fortune cookies, but more like, gratitude cookies.

entry.title: Gardening & Lights
entry.content: In 2017 I began planning my garden. In 2018 I began building my indoor garden. It’s still a work in progress.
entry.summary: In 2017 I began planning my garden. In 2018 I began building my indoor garden. It’s still a work in progress.

entry.title: Vermicomposting
entry.content:
Since 2017 I’ve been working on converting all the waste I produce into sanitized building and growing material.

I had, had a mini-worm farm when I was a child and remembered it being easy (I also had successfully grown a garden then too). For some reason as an adult all of these skills were lost to me. It took 3 years to actually produce successful compost. The first worms I brought died from a mite infestation and overwatering. The second batch of worms I ordered were moved into a poorly crafted CFT bin (two rubbermaid buckets slapped together). They survived and I moved them into a new garbage bin design, that worked!! The only problem was scaling up.

Vermicomposting successfully enough to generate income required more space than I had available to me to use. Also the more worms you have the more they need to eat, and we didn't produce enough food waste. Another challenge we had was composting waste quickly, esp. large cardboard -- this latter continues to be a challenge for me.
entry.summary: Since 2017 I’ve been working on converting all the waste I produce into sanitized building and growing material.

entry.title: Martial Arts
entry.content:
6 Months after giving birth to Little Fig, I joined Shaolin & Tai Chi. I often say that little fig gave me life, but this school and the mentor who took me under his wing really helped shaped me. Underneath their guidance and compassion I really grew and blossomed and had learned the foundations for self-discipline. I can only hope that the skills I learned from my time here will not only travel with me, but serve as a foundation for what will be the rest of my life.

My life and interests are and continue to be heavily influenced by XWP, Zoe Bell, the Last Airbender/Legend of Korra and Shaolin Monastery documentaries. I’ve always been determined to discover myself, grow and make dreams happen, and my birthday gift to me was to take that a step further. It came down to two choices. Reach out to a spiritual therapist or a Shaman (I had my heart set on Hudson Leick), or join a martial arts school focused on mind body spirit growth and integration. I could only afford one or the other, so I figured I’d give the school a try and here I am, there I was. My vision evolved and I went from wanting to live in a cave and a tent to – wanting to buy land and live in my monastery. Now my Raison d’etre is to acquire the skills I need to take back to my Guardian monastery.
entry.summary: 6 Months after giving birth to Little Fig, I joined Shaolin & Tai Chi. I often say that little fig gave me life, but this school and the mentor who took me under his wing really helped shaped me. Underneath their guidance and compassion I really grew and blossomed and had learned the foundations for self-discipline. I can only hope that the skills I learned from my time here will not only travel with me, but serve as a foundation for what will be the rest of my life.
